audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Exodus / Shemot priestly ordination text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for priestly consecration, offerings, blood on ear/thumb/toe, seven-day ordination, daily offering, meeting place, and Divine dwelling.

verses.restored: This is the matter you shall do to them to sanctify them to serve as priests to me: take one bull, two rams, complete ones,
and unleavened bread, unleavened cakes mixed with oil, unleavened wafers spread with oil; of fine wheat flour you shall make them.
Put them in one basket and bring them near in basket, and the bull and two rams.
Bring Aaron and his sons near to entrance of tent of meeting and wash them with water.
Take garments and clothe Aaron with tunic, robe of ephod, ephod, breastpiece, and gird him with woven band of ephod.
Set turban on his head, and put holy crown on turban.
Take anointing oil, pour on his head, and anoint him.
Bring his sons near and clothe them with tunics.
Gird them with sash, Aaron and his sons, and bind caps on them; priesthood shall be theirs as age-long statute. You shall fill hand of Aaron and hand of his sons.
Bring bull before tent of meeting, and Aaron and his sons shall lay hands on head of bull.
Slaughter bull before YHWH, entrance of tent of meeting.
Take from blood/life of bull and put on horns of altar with your finger, and pour all blood/life at base of altar.
Take all fat covering inward parts, lobe above liver, two kidneys and fat on them, and turn them to smoke on altar.
But flesh of bull, its skin, and its dung you shall burn with fire outside camp; it is sin/misalignment offering.
Take one ram, and Aaron and his sons shall lay hands on head of ram.
Slaughter ram, take its blood/life and throw on altar all around.
Cut ram into pieces, wash inward parts and legs, put them on pieces and on its head.
Turn all ram to smoke on altar; it is burnt offering to YHWH, soothing scent, fire-offering to YHWH.
Take second ram, and Aaron and his sons shall lay hands on head of ram.
Slaughter ram, take from its blood/life and put on lobe of right ear of Aaron and his sons, on thumb of their right hand and big toe of their right foot, and throw blood/life on altar all around.
Take from blood/life on altar and anointing oil, sprinkle on Aaron and garments, and on his sons and garments; he and garments and sons and garments will be holy.
Take from ram the fat, fat tail, fat covering inward parts, lobe of liver, two kidneys and fat on them, and right thigh, for it is ram of filling.
And one loaf of bread, one cake of oiled bread, and one wafer from basket of unleavened bread before YHWH.
Put all on palms of Aaron and palms of his sons and wave them as wave offering before YHWH.
Take them from their hand and turn them to smoke on altar upon burnt offering, as soothing scent before YHWH; it is fire-offering to YHWH.
Take breast of ram of Aaron's filling and wave it as wave offering before YHWH; it shall be your portion.
Sanctify breast of wave offering and thigh of contribution that is waved and contributed from ram of filling, from what is Aaron's and his sons'.
It shall be for Aaron and his sons as age-long due from children of Israel, for it is contribution from peace offerings, their contribution to YHWH.
Holy garments of Aaron shall be for his sons after him, to be anointed in them and to fill their hand in them.
Seven days shall the priest from his sons wear them, the one coming into tent of meeting to minister in holy place.
Take ram of filling and boil its flesh in holy place.
Aaron and his sons shall eat flesh of ram and bread in basket at entrance of tent of meeting.
They shall eat those things by which atonement was made to fill their hand and sanctify them; a stranger shall not eat, for they are holy.
If any flesh of filling or bread remains until morning, burn remnant with fire; it shall not be eaten, for it is holy.
Thus you shall do to Aaron and sons according to all I commanded you; seven days you shall fill their hand.
A bull for sin/misalignment offering you shall offer each day for atonements; you shall cleanse altar by making atonement upon it, and anoint it to sanctify it.
Seven days you shall make atonement for altar and sanctify it, and altar shall be Holy of Holies; all touching altar shall be holy.
This is what you shall offer upon altar: two lambs, sons of a year, day by day continually.
One lamb you shall offer in morning, and second lamb you shall offer between evenings.
A tenth of fine flour mixed with quarter hin of beaten oil, and drink offering quarter hin wine, for one lamb.
The second lamb you shall offer between evenings; according to morning grain offering and drink offering you shall do for it, as soothing scent, fire-offering to YHWH.
Continual burnt offering throughout your generations at entrance of tent of meeting before YHWH, where I will meet with you, to speak to you there.
I will meet there with children of Israel, and it shall be sanctified by my glory.
I will sanctify tent of meeting and altar, and Aaron and his sons I will sanctify to serve as priests to me.
I will dwell in midst of children of Israel, and I will be to them as Divine.
They shall know that I am YHWH their Divine, who brought them out from land of Egypt so that I may dwell in their midst. I am YHWH their Divine.

translation_notes: Ordination/filling-hand language marks priestly installation; preserve idiom.
Blood/life on ear, thumb, and toe marks hearing, doing, and walking consecrated to service.
Daily offering is tied to meeting/speaking presence, not mere ritual mechanics.
Chapter closes by returning to Exodus purpose: YHWH brought them out to dwell in their midst.
safety_note: Sacrificial material should be handled as ancient ritual symbolism of consecration, cleansing, and presence. Do not sensationalize blood or detach priesthood from accountable service.
